incidents.vector: Intrusion into ADT corporate systems on April 20; attackers exfiltrated a customer database and demanded ransom for non-disclosure.
incidents.impact: Names, phone numbers, addresses, dates of birth, and last four digits of SSNs/tax IDs for an undisclosed number of customers were stolen; the gang claims 10 million records. ADT says no payment data taken and alarm systems were not affected.

incidents.victim: npm / Checkmarx ecosystem (Bitwarden CLI, ast-github-action, kics images)
incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — supply chain (TeamPCP, 'Shai-Hulud: Third Coming')
incidents.vector: Trojanized @bitwarden/cli npm package and poisoned Checkmarx Docker, GitHub Actions, and VS Code extension distributions executed a multi-stage payload that harvested cloud, npm, and GitHub credentials and republished malicious updates.
incidents.impact: Self-propagating worm spread credential-stealing payloads through software supply chains, embedding in CI/CD pipelines and exfiltrating tokens to attacker-controlled C2 (audit.checkmarx[.]cx). Follows September 2025 Shai-Hulud and March 2026 Axios incidents.

incidents.vector: ClickFix social engineering: attackers contacted business leaders via compromised Telegram accounts with fake Zoom/Teams/Meet invites, then instructed them to run a command that downloaded a macOS .bin file ('teamsSDK.bin') deploying the macrasv2 stealer.
incidents.impact: Stealer harvests browser credentials, cookies, macOS Keychain entries, and SaaS session tokens, exfiltrating via Telegram. Targets cryptocurrency wallets and corporate access at high-value FinTech and crypto firms.

incidents.vector: Attackers obtained valid credentials via social engineering and dark-web purchases, logged in as legitimate users, and used permitted administrative functions to delete data and disable thousands of devices.
incidents.impact: Thousands of medical devices reportedly disabled in what officials call the most high-profile Iranian retaliatory cyberattack to date; paired with an information operation amplifying the perceived sophistication of the attack.

incidents.vector: Custom backdoors (LaxGopher, RatGopher, CompactGopher, BoxOfFriends, SSLORDoor) abused legitimate cloud services — Slack, Discord, Microsoft Outlook drafts, and file.io — for command-and-control and data exfiltration.
incidents.impact: ESET confirmed 12 backdoored systems inside one Mongolian government institution, with evidence suggesting dozens more victims. Active since November 2023.

incidents.victim: U.S. federal civilian agency (Cisco Firepower / Secure Firewall users)
incidents.vector: Exploited n-day Cisco ASA flaws CVE-2025-20333 and CVE-2025-20362 in 2025 to deploy the FIRESTARTER backdoor and Line Viper implant, achieving persistence on perimeter firewalls that survived firmware patches.
incidents.impact: FIRESTARTER allowed attackers to regain access in March 2026 without re-exploiting the original vulnerabilities, exposing administrative credentials, certificates, and private keys; CISA issued Emergency Directive 25-03 requiring federal agencies to inventory and forensically inspect Cisco firewalls.

incidents.vector: Attack originated at third-party AI tool Context.ai after an employee was infected with Lumma Stealer (lured via a Roblox cheat search). Stolen OAuth tokens enabled rapid API enumeration of Vercel customer environment variables.
incidents.impact: Attackers traversed Vercel's internal systems, decrypted customer environment variables, and may have access keys, source code, and database credentials being offered for sale. Vercel says a 'small number' of customer accounts were impacted; supply chain integrity reportedly intact.

incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — transnational fraud (Senator Kok An network)
incidents.vector: 'Pig butchering' romance and investment scams operated from compounds in Cambodia and Burma, in many cases using human-trafficked workers, to steer U.S. victims into fraudulent cryptocurrency investments.
incidents.impact: OFAC sanctioned Cambodian Senator Kok An plus 28 individuals and entities; DOJ Scam Center Strike Force seized 503 fraudulent web domains, charged two individuals running a Burma compound, and restrained $700M in cryptocurrency. State Department offered up to $10M for information on Tai Chang compound proceeds.

incidents.vector: Unspecified intrusion that triggered city-wide IT network shutdown on Friday April 17; Leon County disconnected its network from the city as a precaution — language consistent with ransomware containment.
incidents.impact: Several city websites and public services remained unavailable for days. Cause not officially confirmed but containment posture suggests ransomware.

incidents.victim: Microsoft (167 vulnerabilities, including SharePoint Server zero-day)
incidents.vector: Active exploitation of CVE-2026-32201 (SharePoint spoofing) and CVE-2026-33825 ('BlueHammer' Windows Defender privilege escalation). Adobe Reader CVE-2026-34621 actively exploited since November 2025; Chrome's fourth zero-day of 2026 also patched.
incidents.impact: Microsoft April 2026 Patch Tuesday — second-largest on record — fixes 167 flaws; CISA added CVE-2026-34621 and others to KEV. Active exploitation poses immediate risk to enterprise SharePoint, Defender, Reader, and Chrome users.

incidents.victim: Twilio, LastPass, DoorDash, Mailchimp customers (Scattered Spider 2022 victims)
incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — SIM-swap and SaaS extortion (Scattered Spider / 'The Com')
incidents.vector: SMS phishing (smishing) campaign in summer 2022 stole employee credentials at the named tech companies; stolen data enabled SIM-swap attacks against individual cryptocurrency investors.
incidents.impact: Tyler 'Tylerb' Buchanan, 24, of Dundee, Scotland, pleaded guilty in U.S. court to wire fraud conspiracy and identity theft, admitting to stealing at least $8M in virtual currency. Faces up to 22 years in prison. Second known Scattered Spider member convicted.

incidents.vector: ZionSiphon OT-targeted malware checked for Israeli IP ranges and water-utility software paths, then attempted to push pump pressure and chlorine to maximum levels. Discovered by Darktrace.
incidents.impact: Attack failed to fire because of a XOR encoding bug in the IP range check; malware self-deleted. Demonstrates ongoing Iranian targeting of Israeli water infrastructure (consistent with attacks since 2020). Unclear whether the sample was deployed in the wild.

incidents.vector: Attackers exploited a vulnerability in the Kelp DAO smart-contract platform to withdraw funds as rsETH (restaked Ether) tokens.
incidents.impact: $292 million stolen — the largest crypto heist of 2026 to date.

incidents.vector: External-tenant Microsoft Teams chats and vishing impersonating IT helpdesk, convincing users to launch Quick Assist and grant remote access. Followed by DLL side-loading via signed Adobe/Microsoft binaries, WinRM lateral movement, and Rclone exfiltration to external cloud storage.
incidents.impact: Microsoft Defender Experts observed multiple intrusions where attackers reached domain controllers and staged business data for exfiltration; pattern overlaps with Black Basta / Storm-1811 helpdesk-impersonation playbook.

incidents.victim: Ukrainian prosecutors and allied militaries (Greece, Romania, Serbia)
incidents.vector: Targeted email-account compromise of more than 170 Ukrainian prosecutors plus military networks in Greece, Romania, and Serbia; campaign first surfaced by Ctrl-Alt-Intel.
incidents.impact: Russian military-intelligence operators gained access to investigative materials and military communications across Ukraine and three NATO/aligned states.

incidents.vector: Unattributed wallet compromise; Grinex blamed 'Western intelligence agencies' but provided no evidence.
incidents.impact: $13 million (approximately 1 billion rubles) drained; exchange announced it was shutting down. Grinex was sanctioned by the U.S. in August 2025 for sanctions evasion and ransomware-payment laundering.

incidents.victim: Cryptocurrency, venture capital, and blockchain professionals (macOS users)
incidents.attacker: North Korea — state-sponsored, financially motivated (Sapphire Sleet)
incidents.vector: Fake recruiter profiles led targets to download a 'Zoom SDK Update.scpt' AppleScript that staged multi-stage payloads (com.apple.cli host monitor, systemupdate.app credential phisher) via curl chains, harvested macOS Keychain data, and persisted via launch daemons.
incidents.impact: User-initiated execution sidestepped TCC, Gatekeeper, and notarization. Microsoft and Apple coordinated detections and infrastructure blocks. Ongoing risk to crypto and finance sector employees worldwide.

incidents.vector: Sophisticated distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) flood that intensified throughout April 16; attackers continued throughout the week with smaller follow-ons.
incidents.impact: Bluesky operated intermittently for nearly a day on April 15-16, with degraded access for users globally. Mastodon's flagship server was hit in a similar wave around the same time.

incidents.victim: Microsoft Office customers on more than 18,000 networks (200+ organizations)
incidents.attacker: Russia — GRU military intelligence (Forest Blizzard / APT28)
incidents.vector: Exploited known vulnerabilities in older MikroTik and TP-Link SOHO routers to alter DNS settings, redirecting Office authentication traffic to attacker-controlled DNS servers and silently siphoning OAuth tokens — no malware installed on victim hosts.
incidents.impact: Black Lotus Labs and Microsoft identified more than 200 organizations and 5,000 consumer devices in the dragnet, including foreign-affairs and law-enforcement ministries. UK NCSC issued a parallel advisory on Russian router compromises.
